commit.message:
Sprint 29b: fix P0 Alt+key, P0 drag phase, P1 press/release, P1 PollEvent latency

- Input.fs: Add MousePhase DU (Pressed | Released | Motion)
- Input.fs: Add Phase field to MouseEvent record (breaking change, all sites updated)
- Input.fs: parseSgrMouse — set Phase from M/m terminator + btn&32 motion flag
- Input.fs: parseSgrMouse — strip bit 32 before button classification (drag no longer misclassifies)
- Input.fs: parseEscape — Alt+key single-char case now returns KeyPressed+Alt (no longer falls to None/bare Escape)
- Detect.fs: Add SemaphoreSlim (eventSignal) — PollEvent wakes instantly when event arrives
- Detect.fs: Downgrade ?1002h → ?1000h only (button-event motion disabled until Motion is wired end-to-end)
- Testing.fs: clickAt uses Phase = Pressed
- InputTests.fs: 54 tests — press/release distinction, drag motion events, Alt+key via defaultValue path, scroll+modifier property, motion-bit identity property
- Tests.fs: Update two MouseEvent record literals with Phase field
- 1,640 tests passing (was 1,627)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[docs] docs: document Vanilla CSI N/A behavior in update table (#2908)

* docs: document Vanilla CSI N/A update behavior

Kind bundles local-path-provisioner by default, so the KSail detector
cannot distinguish Kind's bundled CSI from a KSail-installed CSI driver.
The diff engine skips CSI comparison for Vanilla (and VCluster) entirely
to prevent false-positive diffs — CSI config changes have no effect
during `ksail cluster update` for Vanilla clusters.

Update the support-matrix Update Behavior table to reflect this:
- Change Vanilla CSI from 'In-place' to 'N/A²'
- Add footnote ² with a clear explanation and workaround (recreate cluster)

Follows the same fix applied in #2855 to resolve false-positive diffs.

Update docs and add warning for strictMatching (#8388)

This PR adds `strictMatching` runtime warning and documentation for
`@azure/msal-angular`. When `strictMatching` is not explicitly
configured on `MsalInterceptorConfiguration`, `MsalInterceptor` now
emits a one-time warning via the MSAL logger. This helps developers
discover silent 401 failures caused by misconfigured
`protectedResourceMap` keys under the v5 default strict matching
behaviour.

#### Code Changes
- **`msal.interceptor.ts`** — Inline check in the constructor emits a

Add Zorn GF(2)/GF(3) puzzle prototypes and analysis

Explore non-associative loop constructions for puzzle design.

Key findings:
- Chein M(G,2) loops are fatally flawed: 100% sequential reachability
  means non-associativity is completely avoidable (bubble sort solves it)
- Zorn GF(3) loop (2160 states) has only 5% sequential reachability,
  meaning 95% of scrambles force parenthesized expressions
- The cross product mod q is the sole source of non-associativity
- Zorn GF(2) (120 states, 80% forced) serves as a warm-up

Files:
- puzzle_zorn2.py: GF(2) puzzle, 120 states (warm-up)
- puzzle_zorn3.py: GF(3) puzzle, 2160 states (full challenge)
- zorn_gf3_reachability.py: sequential reachability analysis
- README.md: full analysis writeup

commit.message:
feat: async throw, cross-frame exceptions, Error.captureStackTrace, TypedArray (#451)

* fix: async functions now return rejected promises on throw

Previously, JavaScript exceptions thrown inside an async function body
(e.g. \sync function f() { throw new Error(); }\) propagated as Rust
errors via the \?\ operator in \un_async_function\. Per §27.7.5.2,
these should be caught and surfaced as rejected promises.

Changes:
- Catch JS-level errors (TypeError, ReferenceError, JsException, etc.)
  in run_async_function and convert them to rejected promises.
- Handle awaiting rejected promises: extract the rejection reason and
  reject the outer async-function promise (§27.7.5.3).
- Handle awaiting pending promises gracefully (no event loop yet).
- Add js_error_to_rejection_reason helper that maps JS exception types
  to JsValue while letting engine errors (OOM, sandbox) propagate.
- Add tests: async throw → rejected promise, error classification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: cross-frame exception handling and exception conformance tests

- Add PENDING_EXCEPTION thread-local to preserve thrown JsValue across
  frame boundaries. Previously, thrown values were serialized to strings
  when propagating as StatorError::JsException, so outer try/catch blocks
  could not catch exceptions thrown by called functions.

- Expose Error.captureStackTrace and Error.stackTraceLimit as properties
  on the Error constructor (V8 extension). Error is now a PlainObject
  with __call__ instead of a plain NativeFunction.

- Only consume the pending exception slot when a handler actually exists
  in the current frame, preventing intermediate frames from eating the
  value.

- Add comprehensive exception handling conformance tests:
  - throw 42, throw "message", throw new Error(), throw {custom: true}
  - try/catch/finally exception path (catch + finally both run)
  - finally runs after return in try body
  - re-throw from catch propagates correctly
  - for-in with break inside try triggers finally
  - cross-frame throw preserves original value (number, string)
  - Error.captureStackTrace and Error.stackTraceLimit exist on Error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable DatabaseSuitability tests by removing broken CometAdapter configure-time check (#8488)

* Initial plan

* Fix CometAdapter binary detection - remove configure-time check

CometAdapter is a built TOPP tool, not an external binary. Removed OPENMS_FINDBINARY check that always failed at configure time. DatabaseSuitability tests now properly enabled when Comet binary is available.

Co-authored-by: jpfeuffer <8102638+jpfeuffer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add PATH environment for DatabaseSuitability tests to find CometAdapter

DatabaseSuitability calls CometAdapter via ExternalProcess::run(), which searches for executables in PATH. Added ENVIRONMENT property to tests so TOPP_BIN_PATH is prepended to PATH, allowing CometAdapter to be found at runtime.

Co-authored-by: jpfeuffer <8102638+jpfeuffer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve comment explaining PATH requirement for DatabaseSuitability tests

Co-authored-by: jpfeuffer <8102638+jpfeuffer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: relax FuzzyDiff tolerance for DatabaseSuitability tests

DatabaseSuitability uses random subsampling (getSubsampledFasta_ at 50%
rate) for correction_factor calculation, making results non-deterministic
across runs. The default FuzzyDiff tolerance (1% ratio) is too tight,
causing intermittent test failures. Use 5% ratio/absolute tolerance to
account for inherent variation while still catching regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: escape semicolons in PATH for Windows ENVIRONMENT property

On Windows, semicolons in PATH values are interpreted as CMake list
separators when used in set_tests_properties(ENVIRONMENT ...), causing
DLL resolution to fail (exit code 0xc0000135). Escape them properly
and use a single TOPP_PATH_ENV variable for all DatabaseSuitability tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Sprint 29: mouse input — AnsiParser, background stdin reader, SGR mouse mode

AnsiParser module (Input.fs):
- parseSgrMouse: pure SGR mouse CSI body parser, 0-indexed coords, button/modifier extraction
- isCompleteEscSeq: VT sequence completeness check (CSI, SS3, Alt+key forms)
- parseEscape: maps escape sequences to TerminalEvents (arrows, nav, F-keys, SGR mouse)
- All pure functions, no I/O — fully testable in isolation

Backend.create (Detect.fs):
- Background stdin reader replacing Console.ReadKey polling
  - rawThread: blocks on Console.In.Read(), enqueues chars to ConcurrentQueue
  - parseThread: accumulates chars, handles ESC sequences with 50ms timeout
  - Ctrl-A..Z mapped to Key.Char + Modifiers.Ctrl
  - Surrogate pairs and NUL filtered
- ConcurrentQueue<TerminalEvent> replaces Console.KeyAvailable polling
- PollEvent: non-blocking dequeue from event queue (no more CPU spin)
- EnterRawMode: starts reader threads once, writes '\x1b[?1000h\x1b[?1002h\x1b[?1006h' (SGR mouse)
- LeaveRawMode: writes '\x1b[?1006l\x1b[?1002l\x1b[?1000l' before restoring terminal

Windows raw mode (Detect.fs):
- Added ENABLE_MOUSE_INPUT (0x0010) and ENABLE_VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_INPUT (0x0200)
- ConPTY now passes VT mouse sequences through Console.In

InputTests.fs (new, 41 tests):
- parseSgrMouseTests: 16 unit + 2 property tests (coord round-trip, scroll direction)
- isCompleteEscSeqTests: 9 tests (CSI complete/incomplete, SS3, Alt+key)
- parseEscapeTests: 11 tests (arrows, nav, F-keys, SGR mouse via parseEscape)
- mouseDispatchTests: 3 App-level tests via TestBackend verifying App.fs wiring

Mouse wiring in App.fs was already complete (lines 393-397 dispatch MouseInput to
MouseSub and ClickSub). No App.fs changes needed.

Tests: 1,627 total (1,586 existing + 41 new), 0 failed

* fix: use preprocessExpiresField for create-pull-request integer expires conversion

The parsePullRequestsConfig function only converted expires values to hours
when they were strings (e.g., "14d"), but skipped conversion for integer
values (e.g., expires: 14). This caused integer values (representing days)
to be stored as-is, leading to:

1. Overly aggressive maintenance schedule (every 2h instead of daily)
2. Potential premature PR closure (14 hours instead of 14 days)

Replace the string-only preprocessing with preprocessExpiresField(), which
handles all types (integers, strings, booleans) consistently with how
parseIssuesConfig and parseDiscussionsConfig already handle expires.

commit.message:
fix: concurrent task execution safety (6 issues)

Backend:
- C1: Atomic task claim (claim_task_for_result_processing) prevents
  callback + polling from double-processing the same Kling task.
  Uses UPDATE WHERE status NOT IN terminal_states pattern.
- H2: Credit settlement retry (3 attempts + exponential backoff)
  prevents permanent credit freeze on transient failures.
- M2: asyncio.Lock on model config cache refresh prevents
  concurrent DB queries on TTL expiry.

Frontend:
- C2: SSE event handlers now check _requestSeq to discard events
  from stale streams after switchThread.
- M1: Module-level _sendLock prevents double-submit in the tiny
  race window before isStreaming is set.

[RTL] KV error code and write entry bug fixes (#1203)

* update kv read client to ensure that error codes are captured even during read operations
update swwe for write controls so that they can't be modified during kv writes

* [VAL] Add KV SWWE lock test and assertions

Add smoke_test_kv_swwe_lock to validate that KV read/write control
registers are locked by SWWE while crypto engines are busy. The test
exercises three scenarios:

- HMAC and ECC KV WR_CTRL registers (write_entry, dest_valid) reject
  FW writes while busy_o is asserted, then accept writes after idle
- KV RD_KEY/BLOCK/SEED_CTRL registers reject FW writes while busy
- pcr_hash_extend field in KV read controls is permanently locked
  (SWWE=0) for HMAC, ECC, and AES engines

- write_entry and write_en stability while busy for HMAC, ECC, AES,
  and SHA512 engines
- kv_read_client error_code captures read_allow failures with correct
  priority (KV_READ_FAIL on next cycle)

error_code fix. The test injects a valid key into a KV slot, triggers
an HMAC KV read, then sets lock_use mid-read (after validated_read_en
de-asserts) to verify that kv_resp.error on beats >0 is correctly
captured as KV_READ_FAIL. Before the fix, error detection was gated
by validated_read_en (one-cycle pulse) so mid-read errors were missed.

Also removes the lock_use test (TEST 4) from smoke_test_kv_swwe_lock

commit.message:
fix(backend): use Google public DNS (8.8.8.8) for domain TXT verification

AWS Lambda runs in a VPC where the internal DNS resolver (169.254.100.5)
may return NXDOMAIN for newly-added public TXT records, even when the
record is visible on public resolvers. Replace net.LookupTXT() (which
uses the system/VPC resolver) with a custom net.Resolver dialing
8.8.8.8:53 with a 10-second timeout.

This fixes _rootaccess-verify.<custom-domain> lookups always failing
from Lambda regardless of whether the user has correctly added the TXT
record at their registrar.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
